feature: Inventory and dependency graph: metric storage / cost

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for metric storage: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.

interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for metric storage.
cost_opportunity: Identify savings opportunities for metric storage, quantify estimated monthly impact, confidence, effort, risk, and verification steps.

rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling metric storage inventory broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for metric storage.
acceptance_criteria: Given metric storage data exists, when the cost inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.

cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for archive export recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.

docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for archive export.
acceptance_criteria: Given archive export data exists, when the scalability inventory workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for telemetry backup: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Let an agent investigate telemetry backup operational-excellence issues with bounded tool use, read-only diagnostics first, explicit uncertainty, and approval-gated mutations.
backend_scope: Add tool registry, planner, execution trace, read-only default policy, token/time budgets, rollback plan schema, and stop conditions for telemetry backup.
frontend_scope: Add investigation timeline, tool-call evidence, confidence labels, approval prompts, replay, and promote-to-runbook actions for telemetry backup.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Telemetry Storage and Retention telemetry backup agentic-investigation with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: telemetry-backup_operational-excellence_agentic-investigation evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for telemetry backup operational-excellence that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for telemetry backup; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for telemetry backup.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for telemetry backup recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for telemetry backup.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for telemetry backup.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing telemetry backup data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling telemetry backup agentic-investigation broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for telemetry backup.
acceptance_criteria: Given telemetry backup data exists, when the operational-excellence agentic-investigation workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: telemetry backup connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging

ship_slice: Ship as one vertical slice for evidence immutability: data contract, collector or tool adapter, backend API, deterministic evaluator, UI surface, tests, documentation, and operator runbook.
implementation_detail: Support read, validate, mutate, rollback, and audit workflows for evidence immutability with security guardrails.
backend_scope: Add validated commands, dry-run support, RBAC checks, and audit records for evidence immutability.
frontend_scope: Add action menu, confirmations, diff previews, and result status for evidence immutability.
api_contract: Expose authenticated API endpoints for Telemetry Storage and Retention evidence immutability operations with pagination, freshness metadata, audit IDs, error codes, and export support.
data_model: evidence-immutability_security_operations evidence, score, finding, recommendation, owner, suppression, audit event
deterministic_scope: Implement deterministic checks for evidence immutability security that can run without an LLM, emit reason codes, and preserve raw evidence.
agentic_scope: Allow agentic investigation only through registered tools for evidence immutability; read-only diagnostics are default, mutations require explicit approval, and every tool call is replayable.
interaction_model: Support inspect, compare, diagnose, dry-run, approve, execute, rollback-note, audit, and export workflows for evidence immutability.
cost_opportunity: Record any cost side effect for evidence immutability recommendations so non-cost changes do not create hidden spend regressions.
risk_controls: Require RBAC, scoped credentials, rate limits, stale-data detection, blast-radius summary, suppression policy, and rollback or recovery notes for evidence immutability.
telemetry_contract: Emit collection duration, freshness age, success/failure counts, rule evaluation count, AI/tool-call count when applicable, and user action audit metrics for evidence immutability.
test_plan: Add unit tests for evaluator logic, fixture tests for collected evidence, API contract tests, UI render/action tests, and one failure-path test for stale or missing evidence immutability data.
rollout_guardrail: Gate behind a feature flag, support read-only mode first, document required permissions, and add rollback or disable instructions before enabling evidence immutability operations broadly.
docs_runbook: Document prerequisites, permissions, setup, known limits, common failure modes, triage commands, remediation approval path, and verification checks for evidence immutability.
acceptance_criteria: Given evidence immutability data exists, when the security operations workflow runs, then Mayyam stores evidence, shows status, explains gaps, and exposes API/UI tests for the happy path and one failure path.
dependency: evidence immutability connector credentials, normalized resource identity, sync-run freshness, RBAC, audit logging
